data.content:
I've added TradingView-style charting to Axiomic, validated live in the browser. All math stays in the Rust core per project convention.

## What was added

**Rust core** (`core/src/indicators.rs`, `lib.rs`) — a `heikin_ashi` transform (with a unit test) exposed through WASM and surfaced in [engine.ts](web/src/engine.ts) as `engine.heikinAshi()`. WASM bundle rebuilt.

**Chart UI** — a full rewrite of [CandleChart.tsx](web/src/components/CandleChart.tsx) plus new [ChartToolbar.tsx](web/src/components/ChartToolbar.tsx) and [lib/chart.ts](web/src/lib/chart.ts):

- **Chart types**: Candles / Bars / Line / Area / Heikin-Ashi switcher (HA computed in Rust).
- **Volume histogram** pane (up/down colored, bottom overlay), toggleable.
- **Crosshair legend** overlay showing O/H/L/C, change %, and live indicator values (SMA/EMA/BB) that follow the cursor.
- **Toolbar**: linear/log/percent price scale, fit-to-content, crosshair on/off, volume toggle, and PNG export (stacks main + RSI + MACD panes into one image).
- **MACD sub-pane** (line + signal + colored histogram), time-synced with the main and RSI panes.
- **Drawing tools**: trend lines (two-click with rubber-band preview) and horizontal lines, with click-to-remove and a Clear button. Drawings reproject correctly on pan/zoom/resize.

## Verification

- `cargo test` (core): all pass, including the new Heikin-Ashi test.
- `pnpm lint` (tsc) and `pnpm build`: pass; charts stay code-split (162 kB chunk).
- `pnpm test`: 68 existing + 6 new chart-helper tests pass.
- Browser smoke test: confirmed candles, overlays, volume, legend, RSI/MACD panes, Heikin-Ashi transform, and the drawing layer all render and behave correctly.

Note: I left the right-side `IndicatorPanel` as the source of truth for which indicators (RSI/MACD/Bollinger) are active — toggling MACD there now also drives the new MACD pane. I did not add docs since changes-docs weren't requested; let me know if you'd like a `docs/features/` page per the repo's feature-docs convention.

{"content": "# Chart Timeframes

> The `1D` / `1W` / `1M` / `3M` / `1Y` / `ALL` buttons are TradingView-style
> zoom/lookback presets over the **full** price history — every candle stays
> loaded and draggable.

## Summary

Each timeframe button sets the **visible window** of the chart to a trailing
lookback (1 day … 1 year, or the whole series for `ALL`). The complete daily
history is always loaded into the chart, so you can pan or zoom out from any
preset to reveal every available bar.

## Status

- **Fixed** — 2026-06-21

## How to use

In the web app, click a timeframe button above the chart:

- `1D` — last day · `1W` — last 7 days · `1M` — last 30 days
- `3M` — last 90 days · `1Y` — last 365 days
- `ALL` — fit the entire available history

`1Y` is the default. Drag horizontally to pan further back than the preset
window; scroll / pinch to zoom. The full history is reachable from any preset.

```ts
// App.tsx — the timeframe is a view preset, not an aggregation; all candles
// are passed straight through to the chart.
const candles = allCandles;
<CandleChart candles={candles} indicators={indicators} symbol={activeSymbol} timeframe={timeframe} />
```

```ts
// lib/timeframe.ts — maps a preset to a visible time window over the history.
const range = visibleRangeFor(candles, '1Y'); // { from, to } in UNIX seconds, or null for ALL
```

## Notes / caveats

- Previously the buttons **resampled** the history into bar intervals
  (weekly/monthly/…). On ~1.4 years of daily data, `1Y` aggregated down to only
  1–2 yearly candles — which looked like "the chart shows 1–2 candles." The
  buttons now window the data instead of aggregating it, so a year shows ~250
  daily candles.
- The window is applied as a **logical (bar-index) range**, and the time scale
  uses a small `minBarSpacing`, so a full year fits reliably even in a narrow
  pane (a plain time range would otherwise be clamped to a handful of fat bars).
- Manually panning/zooming moves away from the preset window; click a timeframe
  again to snap back. `visibleRangeFor` keeps at least `MIN_VISIBLE_BARS`
  visible so tiny windows never collapse to a single candle.
- Assumes source candles are sorted ascending by time (they are, as produced by
  the data layer and sample generator).

arguments.content:
# Chart Timeframes

> The `1D` / `1W` / `1M` / `3M` / `1Y` / `ALL` buttons are TradingView-style
> zoom/lookback presets over the **full** price history — every candle stays
> loaded and draggable.

## Summary

Each timeframe button sets the **visible window** of the chart to a trailing
lookback (1 day … 1 year, or the whole series for `ALL`). The complete daily
history is always loaded into the chart, so you can pan or zoom out from any
preset to reveal every available bar.

## Status

- **Fixed** — 2026-06-21

## How to use

In the web app, click a timeframe button above the chart:

- `1D` — last day · `1W` — last 7 days · `1M` — last 30 days
- `3M` — last 90 days · `1Y` — last 365 days
- `ALL` — fit the entire available history

`1Y` is the default. Drag horizontally to pan further back than the preset
window; scroll / pinch to zoom. The full history is reachable from any preset.

```ts
// App.tsx — the timeframe is a view preset, not an aggregation; all candles
// are passed straight through to the chart.
const candles = allCandles;
<CandleChart candles={candles} indicators={indicators} symbol={activeSymbol} timeframe={timeframe} />
```

```ts
// lib/timeframe.ts — maps a preset to a visible time window over the history.
const range = visibleRangeFor(candles, '1Y'); // { from, to } in UNIX seconds, or null for ALL
```

## Notes / caveats

- Previously the buttons **resampled** the history into bar intervals
  (weekly/monthly/…). On ~1.4 years of daily data, `1Y` aggregated down to only
  1–2 yearly candles — which looked like "the chart shows 1–2 candles." The
  buttons now window the data instead of aggregating it, so a year shows ~250
  daily candles.
- The window is applied as a **logical (bar-index) range**, and the time scale
  uses a small `minBarSpacing`, so a full year fits reliably even in a narrow
  pane (a plain time range would otherwise be clamped to a handful of fat bars).
- Manually panning/zooming moves away from the preset window; click a timeframe
  again to snap back. `visibleRangeFor` keeps at least `MIN_VISIBLE_BARS`
  visible so tiny windows never collapse to a single candle.
- Assumes source candles are sorted ascending by time (they are, as produced by
  the data layer and sample generator).

## Source

- [web/src/lib/timeframe.ts](../../web/src/lib/timeframe.ts) — `visibleRangeFor`, `TIMEFRAME_DAYS`, `TIMEFRAMES`
- [web/src/App.tsx](../../web/src/App.tsx) — timeframe state + buttons, passes `timeframe` to the chart
- [web/src/components/CandleChart.tsx](../../web/src/components/CandleChart.tsx) — applies the window as a logical range; `minBarSpacing`

{"content": "# TradingView-Style Charts

> Candles / Bars / Line / Area / Heikin-Ashi, a volume histogram, a
> crosshair-following OHLC + indicator legend, RSI & MACD sub-panes, drawing
> tools, price-scale modes, and PNG export — built on TradingView's
> `lightweight-charts`.

## Summary

The center chart is a full, TradingView-style charting surface. A toolbar above
the chart switches the price-series type, price-scale mode, volume, crosshair,
and drawing tools, and exports the chart as a PNG. RSI and MACD render in linked
sub-panes that stay time-synced with the main chart. All price math — including
the Heikin-Ashi transform — runs in the Rust/WASM core.

## Status

- **Added** — 2026-06-21

## How to use

Open the web app, select a symbol, and use the toolbar above the chart:

- **Chart type** (dropdown): `Candles`, `Bars`, `Line`, `Area`, `Heikin-Ashi`.
- **Price scale**: `Lin` (linear), `Log` (logarithmic), `%` (percentage).
- **Drawing tools**: `╱ Trend` (click two points; a rubber-band preview follows
  the cursor), `— H-Line` (click once to drop a horizontal line). Click any
  drawing to remove it, or `Clear` to remove all.
- **Vol** toggles the up/down-colored volume histogram.
- **✛** toggles the crosshair; **Fit** fits all data to view.
- **⤓ PNG** downloads the chart (main + RSI + MACD panes stacked) as a PNG.

The legend in the top-left follows the crosshair, showing O/H/L/C, the bar's
percentage change, and live values for each enabled overlay (SMA/EMA/Bollinger).
Enable **RSI** and **MACD** in the Indicators panel to show their sub-panes.

```ts
// CandleChart is driven by candles + indicator config + the active symbol/timeframe.
<CandleChart
  candles={candles}
  indicators={indicators}
  symbol={activeSymbol}
  timeframe={timeframe}
/>
```

```ts
// Heikin-Ashi candles are computed in Rust and exposed via the engine bridge.
const ha = await engine.heikinAshi(candles); // Candle[] (same shape as input)
```

## Notes / caveats

- **All math stays in Rust.** Heikin-Ashi is `core/src/indicators.rs::heikin_ashi`,
  exposed through `lib.rs` as `heikin_ashi` and surfaced as `engine.heikinAshi`.
  Rebuild the WASM bundle (`pnpm --dir web wasm`) after editing the core.
- Volume uses a dedicated overlaid price scale (`scaleMargins` pin it to the
  bottom ~18% of the main pane).
- The drawing layer is an SVG overlay positioned with the chart's
  `logicalToCoordinate` / `priceToCoordinate`, so drawings reproject correctly
  on pan, zoom, resize, and data changes. The SVG is not part of the PNG export
  (the screenshot captures the chart canvas only).
- RSI/MACD panes are separate `lightweight-charts` instances kept in sync via
  `subscribeVisibleLogicalRangeChange`.

- [web/src/lib/chart.ts](../../web/src/lib/chart.ts) — chart types, ids, PNG export helper
- [web/src/engine.ts](../../web/src/engine.ts) — `heikinAshi` bridge
- [core/src/indicators.rs](../../core/src/indicators.rs) — `heikin_ashi`
- [core/src/lib.rs](../../core/src/lib.rs) — `heikin_ashi` WASM export

data.content:
Both parts are done. Here's a summary.

## 1. Timeframe fix (1Y / all charts showing 1–2 candles)

The buttons were **resampling** the full history into bar intervals, so ~1.4 years of daily data collapsed into 1–2 yearly candles. They're now **TradingView-style zoom/lookback windows**:

- [timeframe.ts](web/src/lib/timeframe.ts) — replaced `resampleCandles`/`bucketKey` with `visibleRangeFor`, returning a trailing time window (`TIMEFRAME_DAYS`); all candles stay loaded.
- [App.tsx](web/src/App.tsx) — stopped resampling, passes the raw history + `timeframe` to the chart (default `1Y`).
- [CandleChart.tsx](web/src/components/CandleChart.tsx) — applies the window as a **logical (bar-index)** range with a small `minBarSpacing`, so a full year fits reliably and you can drag/zoom out to the complete history.

Verified in the browser: `1Y` now shows ~250 daily candles, and dragging reveals earlier bars (legend went from ~144 to ~120 on pan).

## 2. Docs (the feature-docs skill requirement you flagged)

- Rewrote [chart-timeframes.md](docs/features/chart-timeframes.md) for the new windowing behavior (the old page described the now-removed resampling).
- Created the missing [tradingview-charts.md](docs/features/tradingview-charts.md) for last turn's chart features (types, volume, legend, RSI/MACD panes, drawing tools, PNG export, Heikin-Ashi).
- Updated [docs/features/README.md](docs/features/README.md) index + changelog, and refreshed [docs/TESTING.md](docs/TESTING.md) (timeframe/`visibleRangeFor`, chart helpers, and the `heikin_ashi` core test).

Typecheck is clean and all 73 tests pass.
